file_name: GentechHoldingsInc_20190808_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11776814_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: (a) Exclusive Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Distributor Agreement, Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell and distribute the Products to customers that Distributor introduce and confirmed by Company, (in all territory of United State of America (USA) and to render other services as a distributor for Company as set forth herein. Distributor shall limit its activities with respect to the Products to Customers located within the Territory and refrain from selling or otherwise transferring, directly or indirectly, the Products to any person outside the Territory, without the express written consent of Company. Company shall not sell or otherwise supply, directly or indirectly, the Products to any Distributor's Customer's, except by sale through the Distributor. The Company shall not contact any of Distributor's Customer's for any reason, without the prior written approval of Distributor.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Distributor Agreement, Company hereby appoints Distributor the right to sell and distribute the Products to customers that Distributor introduce, and to render other services as a distributor for Company as set forth herein. Distributor shall limit its activities with respect to the Products to Customers located within the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Exclusive' and the clause that states the Company shall not sell or contact distributor's customers, the changed text introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the exclusivity of the distributorship. While the territory is still defined, the exclusivity is removed from the appointment, creating a potential contradiction in enforcement. Also, the removal of the condition after introducing clients, creates another potential conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Distributor Agreement, Company hereby appoints Distributor the right to sell and distribute the Products to customers that Distributor introduce, and to render other services as a distributor for Company as set forth herein. Distributor shall limit its activities with respect to the Products to Customers located within the Territory and refrain from selling or otherwise transferring, directly or indirectly, the Products to any person outside the Territory, without the express written consent of Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'Exclusive' designation and the prohibition on the Company directly selling to or contacting the Distributor's customers. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text, which is essentially a copy of the changed text before edits, does not specify exclusivity and also doesn't disallow the company to contacting any of Distributor's Customer's for any reason, without the prior written approval of Distributor, suggesting that the distributorship is not exclusive and the company can do so, contradicting the original agreement's intent and potentially leading to conflict over sales and customer relationships. This contradicts the original terms of the agreement and impacts the distributor's rights.

perturbation.original_text: (e) Payment. Upon Customer's acceptance of any order, Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer at least Twenty-Five Percent (25%) of the total order price. Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer the remaining balance upon receipt Packing List and Bill of landing documents, from origin in Brazil Farm.
perturbation.changed_text: (e) Payment. Upon Customer's acceptance of any order, Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer at least Twenty-Five Percent (25%) of the total order price. Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer the remaining balance as agreed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the Payment clause 'upon receipt Packing List and Bill of landing documents, from origin in Brazil Farm' but not removing 'Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer the remaining balance', creates an in-text contradiction, since it doesn't define when the distributor should pay, creating a conflict on when the payments should be made
perturbation.location: 2. ORDERS, PRICE, TERMS OF SALE & PAYMENT. (e)
perturbation.contradicted_location: 2. ORDERS, PRICE, TERMS OF SALE & PAYMENT. (e)
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon Customer's acceptance of any order, Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer at least Twenty-Five Percent (25%) of the total order price. Distributor shall submit to Customer via wire transfer the remaining balance as agreed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific condition for the remaining balance payment ('upon receipt Packing List and Bill of landing documents, from origin in Brazil Farm') and replaces it with 'as agreed'. This creates a contradiction because the original text defines a specific payment trigger, while the changed text introduces ambiguity by not specifying when the distributor should pay the remaining balance, contradicting the prior specificity and making it unclear when payment is due according to the contract. The contradicted text includes the same altered payment term that doesn't define when the distributor should pay. This aligns with the 'Omissions' definition as 'essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections'.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall assign to Company, without charge, any rights in the trademarks of Company that may inure to the benefit of Distributor pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise. Distributor shall execute any documents or do any acts that may be required to accomplish the intent of this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall assign to Company, without charge, any rights in the trademarks of Company that may inure to the benefit of Distributor pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Distributor shall execute any documents or do any acts that may be required to accomplish the intent of this Section', creates an in-text contradiction related to the Distributor's actions. There is no obligation for the distributor to sign the document. This omission creates ambiguity and internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor shall assign to Company, without charge, any rights in the trademarks of Company that may inure to the benefit of Distributor pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligates the distributor to both assign trademark rights and execute any necessary documents to accomplish this. The changed text removes the obligation to execute documents, directly contradicting the intent of the section which aims to ensure the trademark rights are properly transferred. This creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction, as the distributor is now only required to assign the rights, but not to take any further action to make the assignment effective, potentially rendering the assignment incomplete or unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction', where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections. The 'Contradicted Text' is essentially a restatement of the first sentence, but the crucial element enforcing that statement has been removed.
